lede: A look at November 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jimi Hendrix and Emperor Xiaozong of Song, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mohsen Fakhrizadeh
events.desc: Iran's top nuclear scientist, Mohsen Fakhrizadeh, is assassinated near Tehran.

events.title: 2009 Nevsky Express bombing
events.desc: Nevsky Express bombing: A bomb explodes on the Nevsky Express train between Moscow and Saint Petersburg, derailing it and causing 28 deaths and 96 injuries.

events.title: New Zealand Labour Party
events.desc: The centre-left Labour Party takes control of the New Zealand government with leader Helen Clark becoming the first elected female prime minister in New Zealand's history.

events.title: San Francisco
events.desc: In San Francisco, city mayor George Moscone and openly gay city supervisor Harvey Milk are assassinated by former supervisor Dan White.

events.title: CARE International
events.desc: CARE (then the Cooperative for American Remittances to Europe) is founded to send CARE Packages of food relief to Europe after World War II.

events.title: RAF Fauld explosion
events.desc: World War II: RAF Fauld explosion: An explosion at a Royal Air Force ammunition dump in Staffordshire kills seventy people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: At the Battle of Cape Spartivento, the Royal Navy engages the Regia Marina in the Mediterranean Sea.

events.title: Spain
events.desc: Spain declares a protectorate over the north shore of Morocco.

events.title: Alfred Nobel
events.desc: At the Swedish–Norwegian Club in Paris, Alfred Nobel signs his last will and testament, setting aside his estate to establish the Nobel Prize after he dies.

events.title: James Pratt and John Smith
events.desc: James Pratt and John Smith are hanged in London; they are the last two to be executed for sodomy in England.

births.name: Emperor Xiaozong of Song
births.line: Chinese Song dynasty emperor from 1162 to 1189

births.name: Bruce Lee
births.line: American-Chinese actor, martial artist, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Ken Russell, English actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Ada Lovelace, English mathematician and computer scientist
Blanche of Castile, Queen of France from 1223 to 1226

observances.text: Christian feast day: Leonard of Port Maurice
